vehicle.transmission: 6-speed automatic with Touchtronic paddle-shift manual mode

label: Money Pit
verdict: Structurally damaged history makes this a risky buy despite its allure and performance.
whatMakesSpecial: A hand-built British grand tourer with a naturally aspirated 5.9L V12, offering a unique blend of luxury and performance.
whyEnthusiastsCare: The DB9 is revered for its timeless design and the visceral experience of a naturally aspirated V12, a combination that's becoming increasingly rare.

ownerVibe.label: Dealer Dressed as Private
ownerVibe.reasoning: Listed by a dealership on behalf of a private seller, with professional sales language and finance options.

carsCoffee.description: A DB9 always draws a crowd with its stunning design and V12 soundtrack.

communityCredibility.description: The Aston Martin DB9 is highly respected among enthusiasts for its design and performance pedigree.

socialStanding: The car that turns heads at every meet and makes even supercar owners nod in appreciation.

specSignificance.note: Naturally aspirated V12 engines are a dying breed.
High-quality leather interior that adds to the luxury feel.

priceVerdict.reason: The structural damage history significantly impacts value, despite the car's inherent desirability.

reasons: Seller leveraging the car's desirability despite damage history.

priceOutlook.reason: Structural damage history will deter collectors and impact long-term value retention.

issues.title: V12 Engine Maintenance
issues.detail: Routine servicing of the V12 can cost upwards of $2,000â3,000 NZD annually.

issues.title: Touchtronic Transmission
issues.detail: Known for expensive repairs if issues arise, with potential costs exceeding $5,000 NZD.

issues.title: Structural Damage History
issues.detail: Unknown quality of repairs could lead to unforeseen issues and devaluation.

steeringFeel.description: Precise and responsive, though not as communicative as the best hydraulic setups.

engineCharacter.description: The V12 roars with a symphony of mechanical sound, delivering power smoothly across the rev range.

dailyComfort.description: Luxurious but firm; the ride can be harsh on NZ's rough roads, and the cabin is snug.

overallFun.description: A thrilling grand tourer that excels on open roads, but the automatic can feel less engaging.

drivingCharacter.summary: A grand tourer that combines luxury and performance, but the automatic transmission dulls the edge.

reasons: Naturally aspirated V12s are becoming rare
Structural damage history limits classic appeal.

regretRisk.reason: The structural damage history and high maintenance costs could lead to buyer's remorse.

marketTrend.reason: Structural damage history will keep values suppressed despite the model's inherent desirability.

redFlags.explanation: This car has been recorded as structurally damaged by NZTA, which can severely impact value and insurability.

modPotential.powerCeiling: Limited due to the V12's already high output and the car's grand touring focus.
firstMods: High-flow air filters to enhance intake sound.
Exhaust system upgrade for a more aggressive V12 note.
modPotential.handlingUpgrades: Bilstein or KW coilovers can improve handling without sacrificing too much comfort.
modPotential.partsEcosystem: Limited aftermarket support in NZ, but international suppliers like RSC Tuning offer performance parts.
modPotential.collectorRisk: Modifications could further devalue this car given its damage history.

questionsToAsk: Can you provide detailed documentation of the structural repairs?
Has the Touchtronic transmission been serviced or had any issues?
What is the service history for the V12 engine?
enthusiastTake: While the DB9 is a stunning piece of automotive art, the structural damage history is a serious concern that could lead to costly surprises.

alternatives.name: Jaguar XKR (X150)
alternatives.whySuited: For those seeking a British grand tourer with a supercharged V8 and similar luxury appeal.
alternatives.howDiffers: Offers a more modern interior and supercharged power delivery with less risk of structural issues.

alternatives.name: BMW M6 (E63)
alternatives.whySuited: Provides a similar V10 thrill with a more robust reliability record and a manual transmission option.
alternatives.howDiffers: The M6 offers a more aggressive driving experience with a focus on performance over luxury.

alternatives.name: Porsche 911 Carrera (997)
alternatives.whySuited: For those who want a reliable sports car with iconic styling and a flat-six engine.
alternatives.howDiffers: The 911 offers a more engaging driving experience with a focus on handling and precision.
